perturbation.original_text: Reseller's customers (the "Customers") are defined as those organizations/enterprises that will market and manage the end-users of the Products (the "End-Users"). Reseller acknowledges and agrees that it does not have the right to assign sub-resellers under this Agreement outside of the Territory, except with the prior written consent of Diversinet.
perturbation.changed_text: Reseller's customers (the "Customers") are defined as any entity that purchases the Products, regardless of whether they directly manage the end-users. Reseller acknowledges and agrees that it does not have the right to assign sub-resellers under this Agreement outside of the Territory, except with the prior written consent of Diversinet.
perturbation.explanation: The definition of 'Customers' is changed to include any entity that purchases the Products, which contradicts the original definition that specifically limits Customers to organizations/enterprises that market and manage the end-users. The change potentially creates uncertainty about who qualifies as a Customer under the agreement. This change would affect Section 5a, where it refers back to 'Customers'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Customer Orders (a) Customer Agreements. The Parties acknowledge and agree that Reseller's Customers will acquire the Products and related services under contract with Reseller, that Diversinet shall not have any contractual interest in Reseller's Customer relationships. Prior to Reseller committing to provide any Products to a Customer, Reseller shall cause Customer to enter into purchase agreement(s) with Reseller with respect to each of the Products purchased by Customer (each, a "Customer Agreement"). Reseller agrees that it shall include terms and conditions in its Customer Agreements that are sufficient to protect Diversinet's interests as reflected in this Agreement, including, but not limited to, those terms set out in Schedule 4 hereto. Such Customer Agreements shall be subject to Diversinet's approval, which approval shall not be unreasonably withheld. Reseller shall use commercially reasonable efforts to ensure that all Customers abide by the terms of the applicable Customer Agreement(s). Furthermore, Reseller shall enforce its Customer Agreements against Customer when requested to do so by Diversinet. For greater clarity, Diversinet expects that the Reseller will include all of the points covered in Section 4 in its end user agreement. When the Reseller receives a customer order they will fax both the end user agreement and the details of the order to Diversinet and if the end user agreement contains all of the points outlined in Section 4 then Diversinet will approve the order and ship the required products to the Reseller.
perturbation.justification: The original text defined 'Customers' as organizations/enterprises that market and manage end-users. The changed text broadens this definition to 'any entity that purchases the Products, regardless of whether they directly manage the end-users.' This directly impacts Section 5a, 'Customer Orders,' because the requirements for 'Customer Agreements' now apply to a potentially much broader set of entities, creating uncertainty about which entities require these agreements. This is an example of ambiguities in in-text contradiction as 'customers' is not defined consistantly

perturbation.original_text: Subject to payment of the Annual Minimum Commitment ("AMC" - defined herein), Diversinet hereby grants to Reseller an exclusive, non- transferable and non-assignable right to market, sell, and sub-license those Diversinet products listed in Schedule 2 (the "Products") within the territory listed in Schedule 3 (the "Territory") to Canadian headquartered companies, and governmental and broader public sector entities located in Canada.
perturbation.changed_text: Subject to payment of the Annual Minimum Commitment ("AMC" - defined herein), Diversinet hereby grants to Reseller a non-exclusive, transferable and assignable right to market, sell, and sub-license those Diversinet products listed in Schedule 2 (the "Products") within the territory listed in Schedule 3 (the "Territory") to Canadian headquartered companies, and governmental and broader public sector entities located in Canada.
perturbation.explanation: The term 'right' is altered from 'exclusive, non-transferable and non-assignable' to 'non-exclusive, transferable and assignable'. This directly contradicts later sections of the agreement that operate under the assumption of an exclusive agreement, notably clauses regarding termination for failure to meet sales targets, and any clause reffering to exclusive rights. This creates a contradiction and makes enforcement problematic.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Assuming that the AMC amounts have been paid and the Reseller has not terminated the AMC, Reseller shall have the exclusive right to contract with Canadian head quartered companies and governmental and broader public sector entities located in Canada (the "Territory"). Furthermore, Reseller will have the non-exclusive right to contract in the rest of the world, excluding the United States.
perturbation.justification: The original text grants the reseller an 'exclusive, non-transferable and non-assignable' right. The changed text alters this to a 'non-exclusive, transferable and assignable' right. This directly contradicts the 'Contradicted Text' which explicitly states that 'Reseller shall have the exclusive right to contract with Canadian head quartered companies'. This change creates a significant in-text contradiction because the exclusivity granted in the contradicted text is nullified by the changed text, leading to uncertainty and potential unenforceability. As per the 'Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction' definition, this inconsistency in the rights granted makes interpretation unclear and creates a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: The Customer or End User shall at all times be responsible for all mobile network data, roaming or airtime charges, SMS related charges, application certification charges or marketing costs and any other fees or levies related to the cellular or wireless network over which the Products are offered and/or provisioned, as well as Customer's own Internet access fees (collectively, the "Network Charges").
perturbation.changed_text: The Reseller shall at all times be responsible for all mobile network data, roaming or airtime charges, SMS related charges, application certification charges or marketing costs and any other fees or levies related to the cellular or wireless network over which the Products are offered and/or provisioned, as well as Customer's own Internet access fees (collectively, the "Network Charges").
perturbation.explanation: The responsibility for 'Network Charges' is shifted from the 'Customer or End User' to the 'Reseller'. This is directly contradictory to the final sentence of the original paragraph, which says 'None of the fees chargeable hereunder include any Network Charges and Reseller shall ensure that Customer shall indemnify and hold harmless Diversinet and Reseller against any such Network Charges that may be levied against them for the operation of the Products.'. Now the Reseller is responsible for them but must also ensure the customer is indemnifying them?
perturbation.contradicted_text: Network Charges. The Customer or End User shall at all times be responsible for all mobile network data, roaming or airtime charges, SMS related charges, application certification charges or marketing costs and any other fees or levies related to the cellular or wireless network over which the Products are offered and/or provisioned, as well as Customer's own Internet access fees (collectively, the "Network Charges"). None of the fees chargeable hereunder include any Network Charges and Reseller shall ensure that Customer shall indemnify and hold harmless Diversinet and Reseller against any such Network Charges that may be levied against them for the operation of the Products.
perturbation.justification: The changed text shifts the responsibility for 'Network Charges' from the 'Customer or End User' to the 'Reseller'. This directly contradicts the statement 'None of the fees chargeable hereunder include any Network Charges and Reseller shall ensure that Customer shall indemnify and hold harmless Diversinet and Reseller against any such Network Charges that may be levied against them for the operation of the Products.' because the Reseller is now responsible for charges they're also supposed to be indemnified against by the customer. This creates a clear ambiguity and direct conflict within the contract, making it unclear who is ultimately liable for Network Charges, fitting the definition of Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction.
